chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presented with typical chest pain associated with electrocardiogram in favor of persistent ST segment elevation (STEMI).
history: The patient's history revealed a rare secretory neuroendocrine tumor of the adrenal medulla, pheochromocytoma, diagnosed based on elevated adrenaline and noradrenaline levels. Cross-sectional imaging highlighted the tumor. The patient's cardiovascular complications included rhythm disturbances, hypotension, shock, acute coronary syndromes, transient ischemic attacks, and cerebrovascular accidents.
exam: Vital signs included a blood pressure of 120/80 mmHg and a heart rate of 80 beats per minute. Physical examination revealed no significant abnormalities.
labs: Investigations included an electrocardiogram in favor of persistent ST segment elevation (STEMI), elevation of cardiac necrosis markers, and a coronary angiography revealing an anomalous origin of the right coronary artery and a pheochromocytoma.
correct_diagnosis: MINOCA revealing an anomalous origin of right coronary artery and a pheochromocytoma
differentials: MINOCA revealing an anomalous origin of right coronary artery and a pheochromocytoma
correct_ranking: MINOCA revealing an anomalous origin of right coronary artery and a pheochromocytoma
